results.title: Loews Corporation AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Loews Corporation is a diversified holding company with a portfolio spanning insurance, energy infrastructure, hospitality, and packaging industries. The company has minimal direct AI initiatives, focusing instead on traditional capital allocation and investment management [1]. As a holding company, Loews primarily operates through its subsidiaries, including CNA Financial (NYSE: CNA), Boardwalk Pipelines, Loews Hotels & Co, and Altium Packaging [2]. Benjamin Tisch was appointed CEO in January 2025, succeeding his father James Tisch as the third generation of the family to lead the company [3]. Despite the lack of public AI initiatives at the parent company level, Loews maintains its strategic focus on generating cash flow and allocating capital across its diverse business portfolio.

results.title: Engineer, Endpoint Security | Careerport
highlights: in the St. Louis area. Who We Are: Founded in 1960, Loews Hotels & Co-operates iconic hotels and resorts across the U.S. Together, our diverse and welcoming teams craft exceptional experiences in iconic destinations. Growth and belonging start here; you’ll be valued for who you are and the goals you have. Whether your next career chapter involves making memories for guests or supporting our properties in our Corporate Office, every role—from Guest Services to Finance, Culinary to IT—offers opportunities to grow and make a meaningful impact. Creating a Team Member experience where you belong no matter what race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status that makes you, you is a daily focus for us. What We Offer: This is a bonus eligible position Competitive health & wellness benefits, 401(K) & company match Paid Sick Days, Vacation, and Holidays, Paid Bereavement, Paid Pet Bereavement Training & Development opportunities, career growth Tuition Reimbursement Employee Hotel Rates, other discounts, perks and more What We’re Looking For: A motivated security professional eager to contribute to the improvement of Loews Hotels’ security posture. An individual with a strong security background, committed to continuation learning and the implementation of innovative solutions. Ready to collaborate closely with other members of the Security Operations and IT Service teams to deploy robust security measures across the enterprise. Serve as a significant contributor to the development, implementation, and maintenance of endpoint security solutions that safeguard against cyber threats and vulnerabilities. Who You Are: A service-oriented team player with strong interpersonal skills Someone who works well in a dynamic, fast paced environment An excellent communicator with an ability to adapt to the communication style of others An IT security enthusiast who is passionate about technology and problem solving A highly motivated and results driven self-starter A continuous learner that stays abreast of industry knowledge and technology Military veterans and spouses are encouraged to apply What You’ll Do: Play a critical role in maturing the security posture of our organization's endpoint infrastructure including the design, configuration, integration and deployment of enterprise security software and infrastructure. Deploy and maintain endpoint security platforms and processes including Anti-Virus, Anti Malware, Encryption, System Hardening, EDR, MDM, Web Content Management, DNS Security, Identity Solutions SIEM and Patch Management. Perform threat analysis, monitor security events for signs of security breaches and triage any suspicious behavior. Assist with the health, performance, stabilization and tuning of all Endpoint Infrastructure Evaluate existing endpoint security solutions and participate in the design of strategies to enhance protection against emerging threats. Partner with senior engineers on the research and analysis of emerging technologies that enhance security capabilities and support current and future business objectives. Participate and contribute on enterprise technology evaluations and roadmap discussions with third party vendors. Assess existing security technologies to establish opportunities for improved endpoint protection controls. Ensure security solutions, policies and procedures are sufficient to meet and respond to threats to our data and endpoint infrastructure. Recommend and provide technical support processes, metrics, and SLAs. Maintain and enforce endpoint security policies and standards in alignment with corporate Governance, Security-Risk and Audit policies, procedures, industry regulation, best practices, and security frameworks (e.g., ISO 27001, NIST, CIS). Support and document the implementation of approved endpoint security software and infrastructure components. Oversee projects from inception to closure ensuring completion

results.title: Most Hotel AI is Stuck in "Pilot Purgatory" But Loews Sees a Way Out - Hotel Operations
highlights:
# Most Hotel AI is Stuck in “Pilot Purgatory” But Loews Sees a Way Out
...
Eighty percent of companies are using AI. Fewer than two in a hundred are making real money from it.
...
Dan Kornick, Chief Information Officer of Loews Hotels, the oldest family-owned hotel company in America, named that gap on stage at the 2026 NYU IHIF conference.
...
“80% of companies employ AI in some form across at least one business function, yet less than 20% are really seeing a real financial impact,” Kornick said. “I would say it’s probably less than 2%.”
...
“I don’t think you want AI,” he told people in the audience. “I think you want a highly-trained team member.”
...
Loews gives AI to the team, not in place of it. Take Chat Your Service, the company’s texting platform. A guest can ask for towels, room service, a dinner reservation, or the best pizza slice in New York. Behind the screen, AI drafts the reply. A person sends it.
...
respond faster, more
...
, and more personalized to guests,” Kornick explained. “But the key is, a Loews
...
AI doesn’t
...
The same logic runs through hiring. Loews built an AI job assistant named Gina. Candidates explore roles, ask about benefits, apply, and schedule interviews entirely over text. Coordination comes off the hiring manager’s plate.
...
hiring managers spend less time on
...
more time on connecting
...
people,” he said.
...
Marketing. Loews runs an AI customer data platform that builds thousands of targeted campaigns, work the team could not do by hand before.
...
“We get thousands of calls a month from people saying, ‘I need my folio,'” Kornick noted. “So now with our AI chatbot, it can validate your information and deliver your folio. It’s a better service and that type of agent delivers ROI.”
...
Construction. Loews is using an AI construction intelligence tool to track builds week by week. The team walks every floor with helmet cameras. They feed in the schedule and the BIM, the 3D model of the property. The AI compares what actually got done in each room, by each trade, against the plan.
...
The first is individual productivity. Copilot went to every manager and above, so they can do more with company data, securely.
...
the ERP, sales and catering
...
The third layer is the toughest. Processes that cross multiple systems and multiple vendors. That is the job for agentic AI, and it is Loews’ bet for the coming year.
...
“Our plan for the next year is to take three major ROI-impact use cases in our business and have an agent run that process across different solutions,” Kornick shared.
...
“AI is only as good as the quality, the accessibility, and the connectedness of your data and the systems underneath,” he said. Fragment your operational, guest, commercial, and financial systems, and AI hands you nice-to-haves instead of the insights that actually move the business.
...
That is why Loews has spent years consolidating systems, data, and vendors. It also changed Kornick’s mind. For most of his career, he favored best-in-class point solutions.
...
time, even if
...
“If AI helps leadership make pricing decisions more effectively, allocate labor more effectively, reduce waste, spot faster, and even improve consistency across properties, that creates a structural advantage.”
...
“On a typical Monday, I’m comfortable with our AI strategy. Tuesday, something new comes up and I’m figuring it out. Wednesday, I get 10 vendors who
...
to all my problems. Thursday, a new capability is pretty cool. Friday, I see
...
strategy. I almost feel
...
Maybe what I
...
“We’re looking at prudent scale and adoption of AI, as opposed to being the first people in the pool,” he said.
...
Five years out, Kornick expects AI to rewire the P&L.
...
“AI will quietly rewire the P&L,” he said. “Fewer manual processes, higher margins, smarter pricing, faster decisions.”

results.title: Daniel Kornick
highlights:
Loews Hotels & Co is luxury North American hospitality company which owns & operates 26 hotels, $1.6B revenue and $400M EBITDA. Responsible for all aspects of technology & business enablement for all central support functions, existing properties and new construction projects. Key focus on creating a flexible, stable and secure technology foundation while enhancing our guest experience, driving operating efficiencies and business growth. Key Achievements:
...
- In less than 18 months, transformed the company to being 100% cloud based across 15+SaaS providers and shutting down our data centers. Loews Hotels was one of the first major chains to achieve this milestone in the hospitality industry. This transformation provided greater flexibility, scalability, reliability, security and a resilient disaster recovery capability while reducing operating & capital costs year over year.
...
- Led major transitions of Financials (Oracle), HR (Workday), CRS, PMS, Telephony and Sales & Catering systems into the cloud.
...
- Implemented new Connected Guest Experience platform to provide greater personalized services to our guests. This included the implementation of a new CRM platform with a single view of our guest and their preferences, system integrations through a new middleware platform to reduce costs, and ability to integrate the communications for the entire guest journey from one system.
...
Rich Siegel and the Hospitality Upgrade team for having me as part of their HU High Five interview series
...
Always a pleasure to speak with Rich, and his insightful questions definitely kept
...
latest edition of the HU High Five, Rich Siegel talks with Daniel Kornick , Chief Information Officer
...
, to find out what keeps a CIO of a
...
and Rich discuss it all, including cybersecurity, the future of the industry,
...
they feel about legalizing marijuana.
...
Loews Hotels & Co is proud to see our Chief Information Officer, Daniel Kornick take the main stage at the NYU IHIF Conference. In a compelling fireside chat, he explored the evolving role of AI in hospitality and how Loews  is leveraging it to empower our team members and elevate the guest experience.
...
"The highest-value AI applications are the ones that improve how the hotel runs every day."  - Dan Kornick Loews Hotels & Co I was only able to catch a handful of the many sessions this week at NYU IHIF but this one is timely and relevant for both Hospitality and Multifamily segments in light of private equity interest in management companies. Daniel Kornick of Loews Hotels & Co. emphasized that the biggest AI opportunities in hospitality may not be guest-facing at all. AI Systems at Work, A Fireside Chat took place on the Main Stage at NYU IHIF, June 2, 2026 with Tanya Pratt from Oracle Hospitality Key themes:
...
• Labor efficiency and productivity gains
...
• Back-office automation in finance and accounting
...
• AI-powered construction intelligence to track project progress
...
• Using AI to surface insights from enterprise data faster
...
• Strategic "war gaming" to help leadership teams evaluate business scenarios
...
Perhaps the most important takeaway: the long-term value of AI won't come from isolated tools. It will come from connecting systems, data and workflows across the enterprise.
...
As Dan noted, management companies that leverage AI more effectively may create a meaningful competitive advantage over time. #NYUIHIF26 #HospitalityInvestor #HospitalityTechnology More coming soon on Hospitality Investor https://lnkd.in/gKGxpcfx
...
And here we go! Looking forward to attending IHIF in New York this week and connecting with industry leaders, innovators, and investors from across the hospitality ecosystem. Excited for the conversations, insights, and ideas that will help shape the future of hospitality. And especially looking forward to my fireside chat with Daniel Kornick tomorrow on the main stage as we unpack “AI systems that work”.

results.title: Hameed MA
highlights:
I bring 12+ years of real-time experience as a Senior Lead Data Engineer and Cloud Solutions Architect, where I have designed, architected, and optimized enterprise-scale, cloud-native data platforms that drive decision-making for banking, financial services, capital markets, retail, and supply chain organizations. I thrive at the intersection of data engineering, Generative AI, and automation, consistently delivering scalable and business-impactful solutions. I have engineered and deployed modern data platforms using Databricks, Snowflake, Redshift, Synapse, and BigQuery, leveraging Delta Lake, Apache Hudi, and Iceberg to establish Lakehouse frameworks with governance and lineage through Unity Catalog. I built and orchestrated real-time streaming pipelines with Apache Kafka, AWS Kinesis, Azure Event Hubs, and GCP Pub/Sub, enabling high-throughput use cases like fraud detection, trading analytics, and payment processing. I develop and automate ETL/ELT workflows using dbt, dbt Cloud, Apache Airflow, Matillion, AWS Glue, Azure Data Factory, and GCP Dataflow, ensuring reliability, scalability, and security. I have migrated legacy on-premise workloads into cloud-native ecosystems across AWS, Azure, and GCP by applying Kubernetes, Docker, Terraform, and CloudFormation to accelerate delivery and strengthen resilience. I reinforced governance and compliance by implementing Promethium, Informatica Data Cloud Management, Collibra, Great Expectations, and Apache Atlas, while streamlining delivery through CI/CD pipelines with Jenkins, GitHub Actions, and GitLab CI/CD. I also advanced automation frameworks to improve efficiency across large-scale data projects. I spearheaded adoption of Generative AI by integrating GPT, BERT, DALL·E, and MLflow LLMOps pipelines to enrich datasets, accelerate insights, and transform enterprise data into actionable intelligence. I delivered Vertex AI solutions for supply chain analytics, improving forecasting, inventory optimization, and logistics operations. I designed and deployed ML models using AWS SageMaker, Azure ML, TensorFlow, and PyTorch to strengthen fraud detection, enhance customer personalization, and support risk management. Skilled in Python, Scala, Java, SQL, and NoSQL, I also develop APIs and enterprise applications with Django, Flask, REST, Angular, React, and JavaScript to meet evolving business needs. As an AWS Solutions Architect – Associate and PMP-certified professional, I lead by mentoring teams, solutioning complex initiatives, and aligning technology with business impact.
...
Experienced AWS Data Engineer with expertise in cloud migration, real-time streaming applications, NoSQL databases, and dbt (Data Build Tool) for building and transforming data models. Proficient in migrating data pipelines to AWS using services like S3, Glue, Redshift, and EMR. Skilled in Python, PySpark, Terraform, and dbt for data transformation workflows, with additional experience in Snowflake, Kafka, and DynamoDB. Focused on delivering scalable, efficient, and secure data solutions in the banking and financial domains. Have extensive experience in IT data analytics projects, and hands-on experience in migrating on-premise ETLs to Google Cloud Platform(GCP)using cloud-native tools such as BIG Query Cloud Data Proc, Google Cloud storage, Composer. Designed and implemented cutting-edge Generative AI datainfrastructure using TensorFlow and PyTorch, ensuring seamless integration of deep learning models into scalable pipelines. TensorFlow's Object Detection API, achieving accurate and efficient object detection tasks. Utilized Jupyter Notebooks to facilitate interactive model experimentation, fostering a dynamic environment for sharing live code, visualizations, and key insights. Utilized Data Build Tool(DBT) for data transformations and modeling in Snowflake. Developed Snowflake database objects and optimized performance.Integrated various ETL/ELT tools to streamline data workflows in Snowflake. Show less

# Data Scientist, GenAI
...
At AIG, we are making long-term investments in a brand-new, innovative Generative AI team, designed to explore new possibilities for how artificial intelligence can be applied in insurance and beyond, and we need your help.
...
With the support and investment needed to explore new frontiers in generative AI, you'll be working alongside talented colleagues, innovating and leading projects that will transform how we manage risk and serve our customers.
...
central to our
...
As a Data Scientist at AIG, you will serve a critical role in the architecture, development, and delivery of impactful enterprise-level Generative AI solutions. We are seeking a highly technical individual contributor with extensive hands-on experience, as we are an applied data science team operating in an agile and production-oriented environment. You will be focused on Large Language Models (LLMs) and Machine Learning (ML), acting as a primary technical driver for our most complex projects.
...
End-to-End Development: Lead the development and successful delivery of data science and generative AI solutions in accordance with business requirements.
...
Production Oversight: Monitor solutions in production to ensure performance, reliability, and accuracy.
...
Technical Collaboration: Partner with cross-functional teams including product managers, engineers, and business leaders to translate requirements into technical reality.
...
Evaluation & Quality: Build robust evaluation frameworks to measure LLM efficacy, manage ground truth dataset quality, and guide the product development roadmap.
...
Architecture: Design scalable ML pipelines and RAG frameworks that integrate seamlessly with enterprise data structures.
...
5+ years of experience in a data science role, with a strong emphasis on NLP and ML, working in an agile production-oriented environment.
...
Proven Delivery: Experience successfully delivering multiple GenAI, analytical, or ML projects from conception through to production.
...
Python Expertise: Strong, expert-level proficiency in Python and its data science ecosystem (e.g., PyTorch, Pandas, Scikit-learn).
...
LLM Mastery: 5+ years of practical experience with open-source Large Language Models (Llama 3, Mixtral, etc.), including prompt engineering, inference optimization, and fine-tuning.
...
GenAI Solutions: 3+ years of experience building Generative AI Solutions, including designing and building RAG frameworks, validation pipelines, observability, and monitoring solutions.
...
Education: Master's degree in data science, Computer Science, or a related quantitative field.
...
Palantir Platform Experience: Hands-on experience or certification in the Palantir platform (Foundry/AIP).
...
Ontology Mastery: A strong understanding of Ontology—specifically how to map complex real-world data entities and relationships into a digital twin framework to power AI applications.

We are looking for an Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning, Vice President with a robust background in Generative AI, Machine Learning, enterprise architecture, cloud platforms, and scalable solution design. You will be responsible for designing and implementing AI-enabled solutions that are secure, resilient, compliant, and aligned with enterprise architecture standards. This role requires strong experience in translating business requirements into end-to-end AI solution architectures, including platform integration, governance, observability, and production readiness. The ideal candidate will work across business, engineering, security, data, and risk teams to deliver scalable and responsible AI solutions for enterprise use cases.

This role is important because it helps ensure AI solutions are designed for production readiness, compliance, interoperability, and long-term business value across the organization.

# Associate Data Scientist, New College Grad - 2026
...
- RRS Group is seeking a motivated and analytical Associate Data Scientist to join our team as part of the 2026 New College Graduate hiring program. This role is ideal for candidates with a strong foundation in data analysis, machine learning, statistical modeling, and Generative AI applications. The Associate Data Scientist will work closely with cross-functional teams to analyze large datasets, develop predictive models, generate actionable insights, and support data-driven business decisions.
...
- The ideal candidate demonstrates technical proficiency in SQL and Python, hands-on experience with machine learning and analytics tools, and familiarity with modern AI-assisted workflows and responsible AI practices.
...
- Develop, evaluate, and maintain descriptive and predictive machine learning models using tools such as scikit-learn, Jupyter Notebooks, Python, R, and/or SAS.
...
- Utilize Generative AI and AI-assisted tools, including LLMs, coding assistants, and AutoML platforms, to improve analytical workflows and insight generation.
...
- Apply Generative AI techniques such as prompt engineering, text summarization, classification, and LLM-assisted analysis in business or research applications.
...
- Follow responsible AI principles, including awareness of data privacy, bias mitigation, ethical AI usage, and model limitations.
...
- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, Economics, or a related quantitative field, with graduation expected in 2026.
...
- Minimum of 2 years of experience in data analysis, quantitative modeling, or data-driven decision-making through academic, internship, research, or professional experience.
...
- Strong understanding of statistics and machine learning fundamentals, including model evaluation techniques.
...
- Hands-on experience with machine learning libraries and tools such as scikit-learn, Jupyter Notebooks, Python, R, and/or SAS.
...
- Exposure to Generative AI technologies and AI-assisted analytical workflows.
...
- Experience with cloud-based analytics or machine learning platforms.
- Coursework or project experience involving NLP, LLMs, or AI-driven analytics.
- Knowledge of responsible AI practices, governance, and ethical AI considerations.
...
- Programming Languages: Python, SQL, R, SAS
- Data & ML Tools: scikit-learn, Spark, Jupyter Notebooks
- Visualization Tools: Tableau, Power BI
- AI/GenAI Tools: LLMs, AutoML, coding assistants
- Statistical Techniques: Regression, classification, clustering, decision trees, predictive modeling
...
- Strong attention to detail and data quality
- Ability to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced environment
- Effective collaboration and communication skills
- Curiosity for emerging AI and data science technologies
- Commitment to ethical and responsible AI practices
